ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 / Wicked Panda — China Chopper-style webshell post-exploit (w3wp.exe / httpd spawning recon shells)
ucs.description: Hunts the characteristic APT41 chain of exploiting a public-facing web/app server (Citrix NetScaler, ManageEngine, Log4Shell, Atlassian, Confluence, Telerik etc.) and using a JSP/ASPX webshell — CrowdStrike calls this cluster Wicked Panda / Brass Typhoon (Microsoft) — to spawn cmd/powershell from the IIS or Tomcat worker process for host recon (whoami, net group, systeminfo, tasklist /svc).
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat*.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","ws_TomcatService.exe","PowerShellHost.exe")) AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","ping.exe") by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest | search NOT (parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" process_name="cmd.exe" cmdline="*appcmd*") | stats count dc(process_name) as distinct_recon_cmds values(process_name) as recon_chain values(cmdline) as cmdlines min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest parent_process_name | where distinct_recon_cmds >= 2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT41 — webshell-spawned recon from IIS/Tomcat worker
let RecentWindow = 1d;
let WebWorkers = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat9.exe","tomcat10.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","ws_TomcatService.exe"]);
let ShellOrRecon = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe","arp.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(RecentWindow)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (WebWorkers)
| where FileName in~ (ShellOrRecon)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// suppress legitimate IIS appcmd / appool diagnostics
| where not(InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("appcmd","iisreset","%windir%\\system32\\inetsrv"))
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            DistinctReconBins = dcount(FileName),
            ReconChain   = make_set(FileName, 25),
            SampleCmds   = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 25),
            ParentCmd    = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
            ParentImage  = any(InitiatingProcessFolderPath),
            AppPoolUser  = any(InitiatingProcessAccountName)
            by DeviceName, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessFileName
| where DistinctReconBins >= 2     // single whoami can be benign; chained recon is the signal
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT41 — webshell-spawned recon from IIS/Tomcat worker (Sentinel via Security 4688)
let RecentWindow = 1d;
let WebWorkers   = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat9.exe","tomcat10.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","ws_TomcatService.exe"]);
let ShellOrRecon = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe","arp.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(RecentWindow)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (WebWorkers)
| where ChildLeaf  in (ShellOrRecon)
| where not(ParentLeaf == "w3wp.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("appcmd","iisreset","inetsrv"))
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            DistinctReconBins = dcount(ChildLeaf),
            ReconChain  = make_set(ChildLeaf, 25),
            SampleCmds  = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
            AppPoolUser = any(Account)
            by Computer, ParentLeaf
| where DistinctReconBins >= 2
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT41 Webshell Recon via IIS/Tomcat Worker Process
id: 7c3e4d11-9a01-44c2-9c4e-apt41-webshell-001
status: experimental
description: Detects host recon utilities spawned by a web-server worker process (w3wp.exe, tomcat, httpd, java) — the post-exploit pattern APT41 (Wicked Panda / Brass Typhoon) uses after dropping JSP/ASPX webshells via T1190.
author: Detection Engineering
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0096/
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0096
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_web:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\tomcat9.exe'
      - '\tomcat10.exe'
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\ws_TomcatService.exe'
  child_recon:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\sc.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
  filter_appcmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'appcmd'
      - 'iisreset'
      - 'inetsrv'
  condition: parent_web and child_recon and not filter_appcmd
falsepositives:
  - Web-app deployment scripts run by an admin shell
  - Monitoring agents that legitimately interrogate the IIS worker
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT41 is repeatedly tied (Mandiant M-Trends, MSTIC, CrowdStrike) to mass exploitation of internet-facing servers (Citrix CVE-2019-19781, Cisco RV320, Log4Shell, ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus, Atlassian Confluence) followed by JSP/ASPX webshells like China Chopper, ASPXSpy and Highnoon webshells. The signature post-drop behaviour is the IIS/Tomcat worker (running as IIS APPPOOL\* or NETWORK SERVI
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 / Winnti — DLL side-loading via signed legitimate binaries (PlugX / ShadowPad / Crosswalk loader pattern)
ucs.description: Hunts APT41's hallmark loader pattern (Winnti Group / Blackfly): dropping a legitimately-signed third-party EXE (Google Update, Symantec, ESET, BMP scanner, VLC, Notepad++ updaters) into a non-standard directory alongside a malicious sibling DLL with a known hijack name (libcurl.dll, log.dll, goopdate.dll, hpqhvsei.dll, vlc.dll). The signed binary loads the unsigned DLL via search-order hijack — the documented delivery method for ShadowPad, PlugX (Korplug) and the Crosswalk backdoor.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("GoogleUpdate.exe","hkcmd.exe","OleView.exe","PotPlayerMini.exe","vlc.exe","BDReinit.exe","oleview.exe","hpqhvind.exe","NvSmartMaxApp.exe","Symantec*.exe","ESET*.exe","acrotray.exe","javaw.exe","chrome_frame_helper.exe") AND NOT (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Program Files\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\*","*\\Windows\\System32\\*")) by host Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest | join type=inner dest [ | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count values(Filesystem.file_name) as side_dll values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("libcurl.dll","log.dll","goopdate.dll","hpqhvsei.dll","NvSmartMax.dll","libvlc.dll","version.dll","vftrace.dll","DSPARSE.dll","WTSAPI32.dll","PotPlayer.dll","libcef.dll") by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_create_time | rename host as dest ] | eval colocated=if(like(dll_path,replace(process_path,"[^\\\\]+$","")+"%"),1,0) | where colocated=1 | stats min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(process_name) as loader values(process_path) as loader_path values(side_dll) as sideloaded_dll values(dll_path) as dll_path by dest user | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT41 / Winnti — signed-loader + side-loaded DLL via Sysmon (Sentinel)
let RecentWindow = 7d;
let KnownLoaders = dynamic(["googleupdate.exe","chrome_frame_helper.exe","hkcmd.exe","oleview.exe","potplayermini.exe","vlc.exe","hpqhvind.exe","nvsmartmaxapp.exe","acrotray.exe","bdreinit.exe"]);
let HijackDlls   = dynamic(["libcurl.dll","log.dll","goopdate.dll","hpqhvsei.dll","nvsmartmax.dll","libvlc.dll","version.dll","vftrace.dll","dsparse.dll","wtsapi32.dll","potplayer.dll","libcef.dll","sensapi.dll","secur32.dll"]);
let ImageLoads =
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(RecentWindow)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
    | extend LoaderImage  = tolower(tostring(EventData.Image)),
             LoadedImage  = tolower(tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded)),
             LoaderUser   = tostring(EventData.User),
             LoaderHashes = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
             LoadedHashes = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
    | extend LoaderLeaf  = tostring(split(LoaderImage, "\\")[-1]),
             LoadedLeaf  = tostring(split(LoadedImage, "\\")[-1]),
             LoaderDir   = strcat_array(array_slice(split(LoaderImage, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\"),
             LoadedDir   = strcat_array(array_slice(split(LoadedImage, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
    | where LoaderLeaf in (KnownLoaders) and LoadedLeaf in (HijackDlls)
    | where LoaderDir == LoadedDir
    | where not(LoaderImage startswith "c:\\program files\\")
    | where not(LoaderImage startswith "c:\\program files (x86)\\")
    | where not(LoaderImage startswith "c:\\windows\\")
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, LoaderImage, LoadedImage, LoaderUser, LoaderHashes;
let RecentDrops =
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(RecentWindow)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 11
    | extend DroppedFile = tolower(tostring(EventData.TargetFilename))
    | extend DroppedLeaf = tostring(split(DroppedFile, "\\")[-1])
    | where DroppedLeaf in (HijackDlls)
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, Computer,
              DroppedFile, DroppedLeaf,
              DropperImage = tostring(EventData.Image);
ImageLoads
| extend LoadedLeaf = tostring(split(LoadedImage, "\\")[-1])
| join kind=inner RecentDrops on Computer, $left.LoadedLeaf == $right.DroppedLeaf
| where DropTime <= TimeGenerated and DropTime > TimeGenerated - 30d
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, LoaderUser, LoaderImage, LoadedImage,
          DropTime, DropperImage, LoaderHashes
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading via legitimately signed binaries is the single most consistently observed APT41 / Winnti technique across reporting from FireEye/Mandiant (M-Trends, APT41 'Double Dragon' report), TrendMicro Earth Berberoka, Kaspersky ShadowPad/PlugX, and Symantec Blackfly. Specific loader/DLL pairs (GoogleUpdate.exe + goopdate.dll, hpqhvind.exe + hpqhvsei.dll, oleview.exe + iviewers.dll, PotPlaye
